pubClosures.note: Cumulative net pub closures in the UK from 2016 baseline. Approximately 6-7 pubs per day closing on a net basis.

nightclubs.note: Number of nightclubs/late night venues in the UK. Down from 3,144 in 2005 — a 72% decline in 20 years.

nightTimeEmployment.note: Estimated employment in night-time economy sectors (hospitality, entertainment, night retail, transport, security), UK.

sources.name: BBPA
sources.dataset: British Beer and Pub Association — Statistical Handbook

sources.name: Night Time Industries Association
sources.dataset: Night Time Economy State of the Industry Report

sources.name: ONS
sources.dataset: Business Register and Employment Survey — Hospitality and Night-Time Sectors

metadata.methodology: Pub closure data from BBPA tracking of licensed premises registrations and de-registrations. Nightclub count from NTIA annual census of late-licence venues. Employment estimate from ONS BRES for SIC codes covering hospitality, entertainment, and related night-time economy activities, adjusted for part-time/evening-only workers.
knownIssues: Nightclub definition is contested — some venues classified differently depending on licence type
Pub closures include permanent closures and conversions to restaurants or other uses — some repurposed buildings remain economically active
Night-time economy employment estimate methodology varies between NTIA and ONS — figures not directly comparable across different publications
2005 nightclub peak figure from NTIA census which used a broader definition than current counts
